reason: While disabling unnecessary authentication mechanisms is standard hardening, Kerberos is often a critical component for enterprise identity management and centralized authentication. Disabling it via SSH configuration could immediately lock out administrators or automated systems that rely on Kerberos for SSH access, especially in domain-joined environments.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential loss of SSH access for users relying on Kerberos authentication; Disruption of automated management tools using Kerberos-based SSH keys/tokens
